passages.text: Abstract Expressionism, a movement that emerged in New York in the 1940s, redefined the nature of painting. Artists like Jackson Pollock and Willem de Kooning moved away from representing objects and instead focused on the process of creation itself. For them, the artwork was not just a final product but a record of an event. The critic Harold Rosenberg famously described this approach by stating that for these artists, “the canvas became an arena in which to act.” This was not painting in the traditional sense; it was a dynamic, physical engagement with the materials. The drips, splashes, and gestures on the canvas were traces of the artist's movements and emotional state, transforming the flat surface into a space of existential struggle and discovery.

questions.text: What is the most likely meaning of the underlined phrase “the canvas became an arena in which to act”?
choices: The canvas was a place for public performances and entertainment.
The artists viewed their work as a competitive struggle against rivals.
The act of painting was a dramatic, physical process rather than just image-making.
The artists used their canvases to stage political protests.
The large size of the canvases resembled a sports arena.
questions.explanation: '행동을 위한 투기장'은 캔버스가 단순히 이미지를 그리는 공간이 아니라, 예술가의 역동적이고 신체적인 창작 행위가 펼쳐지는 무대임을 의미합니다.

passages.text: In Shakespeare's tragedy *King Lear*, the aging monarch descends into madness after foolishly banishing his loyal daughter and bestowing his kingdom upon his two treacherous daughters. As he wanders through a violent storm, stripped of his power and dignity, Lear reflects on his suffering. He recognizes his own poor judgment and acknowledges his flaws. However, in a moment of profound anguish and clarity, he cries out, “I am a man more sinned against than sinning.” This statement captures the central tragedy of his character. While his initial actions were driven by vanity and foolishness, the cruelty and betrayal he endures from his daughters, Goneril and Regan, are disproportionately vicious, pushing him to the brink of human endurance.

questions.text: What does King Lear mean by the underlined statement?
choices: He claims to be completely innocent and free of any fault.
He is accusing the gods of being unjust and cruel to all mankind.
He believes his punishment is a fair consequence of his actions.
He acknowledges his own mistakes but feels the wrongs done to him are far greater.
He is expressing a desire for revenge against those who have wronged him.
questions.explanation: 리어왕은 자신의 잘못("sinning")을 인정하지만, 딸들에게 당한 배신과 학대("sinned against")가 자신의 죄보다 훨씬 더 크다고 한탄하고 있습니다.

passages.text: In an age saturated with information, satirical news programs like *The Daily Show* or *Last Week Tonight* have carved out a unique niche. They do not merely report the news; they deconstruct it, exposing the absurdities and hypocrisies within politics and media. These shows blend comedy with journalism, creating a format that is both entertaining and informative. By using irony, parody, and exaggeration, the hosts cut through the carefully constructed narratives of politicians and pundits. Instead of offering a dry, objective analysis, they wield humor as a scalpel. This approach allows them to dissect complex issues and reveal uncomfortable truths in a way that is highly engaging for their audience, often prompting critical reflection rather than passive acceptance.

questions.text: What does the author mean by the phrase “they wield humor as a scalpel”?
choices: They use comedy primarily to injure the reputations of public figures.
Their humor is often aggressive and causes unnecessary controversy.
They employ humor for precise and sharp analysis to reveal underlying truths.
They believe that laughter is the best medicine for society's problems.
Their jokes are as sharp and dangerous as a surgical instrument.
questions.explanation: '메스'는 정밀한 수술 도구입니다. 이 비유는 유머가 사회 문제의 핵심을 정확하고 날카롭게 파헤쳐 숨겨진 진실을 드러내는 분석적 도구로 사용됨을 의미합니다.

passages.text: Friedrich Nietzsche's philosophy presents many challenging ideas, none more so than the concept of "eternal recurrence." He asks us to imagine a demon whispering in our loneliest loneliness: "This life as you now live it and have lived it, you will have to live once more and innumerable times more." Nietzsche called this thought experiment “the heaviest weight” (das schwerste Gewicht). The idea is not presented as a cosmological fact but as a psychological test. How would you react? Would you be crushed by the thought of endlessly repeating your sorrows and mistakes, or would you be liberated, striving to live every moment so perfectly and meaningfully that you would gladly will its return? The concept forces a radical affirmation of one's life, demanding that we live in a way that we would want to repeat for all eternity.

questions.text: What is implied by Nietzsche’s description of eternal recurrence as “the heaviest weight”?
choices: It is a depressing philosophy that leads to despair and inaction.
It is a scientifically proven theory about the cyclical nature of the universe.
It is a profound and burdensome thought that forces an ultimate evaluation of one's life.
It is an ethical rule that is too difficult for ordinary people to follow.
It is a concept that physically weighs down on a person's spirit.
questions.explanation: '가장 무거운 짐'은 영원회귀라는 생각이 삶 전체를 책임지고 평가하게 만드는, 심오하고 부담스러운 철학적 질문임을 의미합니다.

passages.text: In 1633, the astronomer Galileo Galilei was forced by the Catholic Church to recant his scientific findings that the Earth revolves around the Sun. Under threat of torture, the elderly scientist publicly abjured his own work. However, a popular legend holds that as he rose from his knees after the recantation, he defiantly muttered the words, *“Eppur si muove”*—“And yet it moves.” While the historical accuracy of this event is debated, the phrase has transcended its origins to become a powerful symbol. It represents the resilience of truth in the face of dogmatic authority and the quiet, indomitable spirit of scientific inquiry that persists even when suppressed.

questions.text: What is the symbolic meaning of the legendary phrase “And yet it moves”?
choices: A literal statement confirming the Earth's motion for his fellow scientists.
A quiet admission of defeat and submission to the Church's power.
A symbol of enduring truth and defiance against oppressive authority.
A personal prayer for forgiveness for having lied under duress.
A sarcastic remark intended to mock the intelligence of the inquisitors.
questions.explanation: 이 말은 겉으로는 굴복했지만, 진실은 권력으로 바꿀 수 없다는 내면의 신념과 억압적인 권위에 대한 저항 정신을 상징합니다.

passages.text: The premiere of Igor Stravinsky's ballet, *The Rite of Spring*, in Paris in 1913 is one of the most infamous events in music history. The audience, accustomed to the elegant and lyrical qualities of Romantic and Impressionistic music, was shocked by Stravinsky's jarring rhythms, dissonant harmonies, and depiction of pagan rituals. The performance provoked a riot, with supporters and opponents of the work physically brawling in the theater. The music was a radical departure from the established aesthetic norms of the time. Its primitive energy and brutal force were not just new; they were an assault on the senses and a rejection of the refined past. In essence, *The Rite of Spring* was a sacrificial dance that broke the back of romanticism. It heralded the arrival of modernism in music, a new era defined by fragmentation, complexity, and a raw, unsentimental power.

questions.text: What does the author mean by “a sacrificial dance that broke the back of romanticism”?
choices: The ballet's choreography was physically dangerous for the dancers.
The work marked a violent and decisive end to the dominance of the Romantic musical tradition.
The music was sacrificed because the audience's negative reaction ruined its reputation.
The ballet's theme of sacrifice was a critique of Romantic ideals.
The performance caused a literal breakdown of order and civility in the theater.
questions.explanation: '낭만주의의 등뼈를 부러뜨렸다'는 표현은 이 작품이 기존 낭만주의 음악의 시대를 결정적이고 폭력적으로 끝내고 모더니즘을 열었음을 의미합니다.

passages.text: In his seminal work *The Wealth of Nations*, Adam Smith introduced a concept that would become a cornerstone of classical economics. He argued that in a free market, an individual pursuing their own self-interest can inadvertently benefit society as a whole. A butcher, a brewer, or a baker provides our dinner not from their benevolence, but from their regard to their own interest. Smith famously wrote that in this process, the individual is “led by an invisible hand to promote an end which was no part of his intention.” This "invisible hand" is a metaphor for the unseen market forces that coordinate individual actions, allocating resources efficiently and creating prosperity without the need for central planning or direction.

questions.text: What is the meaning of the phrase “led by an invisible hand”?
choices: Individuals are guided by a supernatural force to help others.
People's actions are secretly controlled by a powerful, hidden authority.
The pursuit of personal gain can unintentionally result in broader social benefits through market mechanisms.
A person's conscience guides them to make decisions that are good for society.
The economy functions best when guided by a wise and unseen government leader.
questions.explanation: '보이지 않는 손'은 개인의 이기적인 이익 추구가 시장 메커니즘을 통해 의도치 않게 사회 전체의 이익으로 연결되는 과정을 비유합니다.

passages.text: In 1917, Marcel Duchamp submitted a porcelain urinal, which he titled *Fountain* and signed "R. Mutt," to an art exhibition. The exhibition committee, which was supposed to accept all submissions, rejected it. This act, and the object itself, sent shockwaves through the art world. Duchamp was not interested in the object's aesthetic beauty or the craft required to make it. Instead, he challenged the very definition of art. By selecting a mass-produced object and placing it in an artistic context, he argued that the artist's idea and choice were more important than the physical creation. *Fountain* was not just a provocative joke; it was a philosophical bomb thrown into the art world. It forced a fundamental re-evaluation of what art is, who decides what art is, and what the role of art institutions should be.

questions.text: What is the best interpretation of the phrase “a philosophical bomb thrown into the art world”?
choices: The artwork caused a violent and destructive riot at the exhibition.
It was an explosive device intended to physically destroy the art gallery.
It was a radically disruptive act that fundamentally challenged the core beliefs and definitions of art.
It was a sudden and loud statement that quickly faded from public memory.
The artwork was universally condemned as a dangerous and immoral object.
questions.explanation: '철학적 폭탄'은 뒤샹의 작품이 예술의 정의, 예술가의 역할 등 예술계의 기존 핵심 신념에 근본적으로 도전한 급진적이고 파괴적인 행위였음을 의미합니다.

passages.text: In her influential 1929 essay, Virginia Woolf explores the systemic obstacles that have historically prevented women from becoming successful writers. She argues that for centuries, women were denied the education, financial independence, and personal freedom that are prerequisites for creative work. They were expected to focus on domestic duties, leaving no time or mental space for intellectual pursuits. Woolf encapsulates her central thesis in a now-famous declaration: “a woman must have money and a room of her own if she is to write fiction.” This is not merely a practical demand for a quiet workspace. The "room" symbolizes intellectual freedom and privacy, while the "money" represents the economic autonomy that liberates a person from dependence and constant interruption, allowing the mind to flourish.

questions.text: What is the deeper meaning of Woolf’s phrase “a room of her own”?
choices: A literal, private study where a woman can write without being disturbed.
A demand for women to own property and real estate.
A metaphor for the intellectual, social, and financial independence necessary for creativity.
A suggestion that women writers should isolate themselves from society.
A critique of the poor housing conditions for women in the early 20th century.
questions.explanation: '자기만의 방'은 문자 그대로의 공간을 넘어, 여성이 창작 활동을 하기 위해 필요한 지적, 사회적, 경제적 독립 전체를 상징하는 은유입니다.

passages.text: In a 1946 speech in Fulton, Missouri, Winston Churchill delivered a stark warning about the growing Soviet influence in post-World War II Europe. He observed that the nations of Central and Eastern Europe, which had been liberated from Nazi Germany, were increasingly falling under the control of the Soviet Union. To describe this new reality of a divided continent, he coined a powerful and enduring metaphor. He declared, “From Stettin in the Baltic to Trieste in the Adriatic, an iron curtain has descended across the Continent.” This phrase vividly captured the new political and ideological barrier that was separating the Soviet-dominated East from the democratic West. The "iron" suggested an impenetrable, oppressive, and permanent division, setting the tone for the decades of suspicion and hostility that would define the Cold War.

questions.text: What did Churchill mean by the phrase “an iron curtain has descended”?
choices: A physical wall was being constructed to divide Eastern and Western Europe.
A new industrial age based on iron production was beginning in Europe.
Europe was being rigidly and oppressively divided into two hostile ideological blocs.
A period of cultural darkness and artistic censorship was beginning.
A military blockade was preventing all trade and travel between East and West.
questions.explanation: '철의 장막'은 제2차 세계대전 이후 유럽이 소련이 지배하는 동구권과 민주주의 서방이라는 적대적인 두 이념 진영으로 엄격하고 억압적으로 분단되었음을 의미합니다.

passages.text: In the world of politics, apologies are rarely simple admissions of wrongdoing. Following the public outcry over his controversial remarks, the senator issued a statement expressing his deep "regret" for any offense caused. However, political analysts were quick to point out that this was not a direct apology for his words, but rather for the public's reaction to them. The statement was meticulously worded to avoid admitting fault while appearing contrite. It was a classic example of a non-apology apology, a tool often used to quell public anger without accepting personal responsibility. For the senator, it was a strategic move in a complex political chess game, designed to protect his image and career. This incident highlights how language can be manipulated, where the stated meaning and the actual intent diverge significantly. The senator’s statement was, in essence, a carefully calibrated expression of regret.

questions.text: What does the underlined phrase "a carefully calibrated expression of regret" imply about the senator's statement?
choices: It was a sincere and heartfelt apology to the public.
It was an emotional outburst made without careful thought.
It was a strategically constructed message to manage public perception.
It was a clear admission of his mistake and a promise to resign.
It was a factual correction of his previous controversial remarks.
questions.explanation: '세심하게 조정된 유감 표명'은 상원의원의 발언이 진심 어린 사과가 아니라, 대중의 인식을 관리하고 자신의 이미지를 보호하기 위해 전략적으로 구성된 메시지임을 암시합니다.

passages.text: Adam Smith's concept of the "invisible hand" has been a cornerstone of free-market capitalism for centuries. It suggests that individuals pursuing their own self-interest inadvertently promote the good of society. The butcher and the baker provide our dinner not from benevolence, but from their regard to their own interest. In Smith's time, this mechanism might have worked well in local, small-scale economies. However, in today's interconnected global market, characterized by multinational corporations and complex financial systems, some economists argue that the hand has become less a gentle guide and more a clumsy giant. Its movements can create massive economic inequality, environmental degradation, and market volatility that Smith might never have foreseen. The pursuit of self-interest on a global scale does not always lead to a harmonious outcome for all.

questions.text: What is the meaning of the underlined phrase "less a gentle guide and more a clumsy giant"?
choices: The market's self-regulating mechanism now operates more efficiently.
The principle of the invisible hand is no longer taught in economics.
The market's invisible forces can cause large-scale, negative consequences.
Individual self-interest has been replaced by a focus on social good.
The global economy is now controlled by a few powerful individuals.
questions.explanation: '서투른 거인'이라는 표현은 현대 세계 경제에서 '보이지 않는 손'이 조화로운 결과 대신, 대규모의 부정적인 결과(불평등, 환경 파괴 등)를 초래할 수 있음을 의미합니다.

passages.text: Dr. Evelyn Reed was one of the most brilliant researchers at the pharmaceutical company, with a track record of successful drug developments. She consistently outperformed her male colleagues and was widely respected for her expertise and leadership. When the position for Director of Research opened up, everyone assumed she was the natural choice. She had the qualifications, the experience, and the ambition. However, the promotion went to a less experienced male colleague. Despite her numerous inquiries, the board's explanations were vague, citing subjective reasons like "fit" and "leadership style." It became painfully clear to her that her qualifications were not the issue. After years of dedicated work and climbing the corporate ladder, she hit the familiar, invisible barrier.

questions.text: What does the underlined phrase "she hit the familiar, invisible barrier" mean?
choices: She decided to quit her job to start her own company.
She failed to pass the mandatory examination for the promotion.
She encountered an unstated, systemic prejudice that blocked her advancement.
She realized she lacked the necessary social skills for a leadership role.
She had a personal conflict with a key member of the board.
questions.explanation: '익숙하고 보이지 않는 장벽'은 '유리 천장'을 의미하며, 뛰어난 자격에도 불구하고 성별 등으로 인해 승진이 막히는 명시되지 않은 제도적 편견에 직면했음을 의미합니다.

passages.text: Sigmund Freud, the father of psychoanalysis, proposed a compelling model of the human mind by comparing it to an iceberg. The small part of the iceberg visible above the water represents the conscious mind—our current thoughts, feelings, and perceptions. This is the realm of our awareness. However, the vast, unseen mass of ice below the water symbolizes the unconscious mind. This submerged part contains our hidden memories, deep-seated desires, and repressed emotions. According to Freud, these unconscious elements, though hidden from our view, exert a powerful influence on our actions, choices, and personalities. He argued that to truly understand human psychology, we must recognize that the true drivers of our behavior lie beneath the surface.

questions.text: What does the underlined phrase "the true drivers of our behavior lie beneath the surface" suggest?
choices: People's actions are mostly random and unpredictable.
Our behavior is primarily dictated by unconscious thoughts and desires.
Conscious decision-making is more powerful than emotional impulses.
Understanding behavior requires observing only visible actions.
Most of our mental capacity remains unused throughout our lives.
questions.explanation: 빙산 비유에 따르면, '표면 아래'는 무의식을 상징합니다. 따라서 이 구절은 우리의 행동이 의식적인 생각보다는 무의식적인 생각과 욕망에 의해 주로 결정된다는 것을 시사합니다.

passages.text: Environmental scientists often use the concept of a "tipping point" to explain the risk of abrupt and irreversible climate change. Imagine slowly leaning over in a canoe. You can lean a certain amount and still recover, but there is a point beyond which the canoe will suddenly capsize, and you cannot stop it. Similarly, the Earth's climate system can absorb a certain amount of greenhouse gases and warming. However, if we push it too far, we risk triggering feedback loops that could lead to rapid, catastrophic changes, like the collapse of ice sheets or the die-off of rainforests. Many scientists warn that with current emission rates, we are dangerously close to tipping the canoe. The window of opportunity to stabilize the system is closing fast.

questions.text: What is the meaning of the underlined phrase "we are dangerously close to tipping the canoe"?
choices: Humanity is becoming more interested in water sports like canoeing.
We are approaching a threshold where climate change could become uncontrollable.
The political will to address environmental issues is finally gaining momentum.
Small, incremental changes are enough to solve the climate crisis.
The danger of climate change is often exaggerated by scientists.
questions.explanation: '카누를 뒤집기 직전'이라는 비유는 인류가 기후 변화가 통제 불가능하고 돌이킬 수 없는 상태가 될 수 있는 문턱(티핑 포인트)에 위험할 정도로 가까워지고 있음을 경고합니다.

passages.text: At the weekly team meeting, the atmosphere was typically tense and unproductive. Team members were hesitant to share their real opinions, and discussions often stalled. Then, a new project manager, David, joined the team. David didn't dominate the conversation or impose his ideas. Instead, he asked insightful questions, actively listened, and connected different people's points in a positive way. Suddenly, the room came alive. People started brainstorming freely, challenging each other constructively, and collaborating on new solutions. David himself didn't propose the final idea, but his presence enabled the entire process. He was the catalyst in the room's social chemistry. Without him, the reaction would never have occurred.

questions.text: What does the underlined phrase "the catalyst in the room's social chemistry" mean?
choices: He was the most dominant and outspoken person in the meeting.
His presence initiated and accelerated positive interaction among others.
He created chemical reactions as part of a science experiment.
He was an outsider who did not understand the team's dynamics.
He was the main source of conflict and tension within the team.
questions.explanation: '사회적 화학 반응의 촉매'는 데이비드가 직접 해결책을 제시한 것이 아니라, 그의 존재와 행동이 다른 팀원들 사이의 긍정적인 상호작용과 협력을 유발하고 촉진했음을 의미합니다.

passages.text: The city's oldest orchestra, founded in 1890, celebrated its 130th anniversary. A music critic, however, wrote a provocative review. He noted that not a single musician from the original ensemble remained. The instruments they played were all modern replacements, not the historic originals. Even the concert hall had been rebuilt twice. The style of interpretation, the repertoire, and the very sound of the orchestra had evolved so dramatically that a founder would not recognize it. The critic argued that while the name and legal entity persisted, the orchestra had become a true Ship of Theseus. He questioned whether it was truly the "same" orchestra, or simply a new one that happened to inherit an old name.

questions.text: What does the underlined phrase "had become a true Ship of Theseus" imply about the orchestra?
choices: The orchestra had successfully preserved its original traditions and sound.
The orchestra's identity was questionable as all its components had been replaced.
The orchestra was named after a famous historical ship.
The orchestra was planning a celebratory voyage on a ship.
The orchestra had lost its funding and was about to be disbanded.
questions.explanation: '테세우스의 배'는 모든 구성 요소가 교체되었을 때 그 정체성이 유지되는지에 대한 철학적 역설입니다. 이는 오케스트라의 모든 것이 바뀌어 그 정체성이 의심스럽다는 것을 암시합니다.

passages.text: In the early days of the internet, it was hailed as a tool for broadening horizons, exposing people to diverse perspectives from around the globe. However, the rise of social media and sophisticated algorithms has led to an unintended consequence. These platforms learn a user's preferences and feed them a constant stream of content that aligns with their existing beliefs. News articles, videos, and opinions from dissenting viewpoints are filtered out, while confirming ones are amplified. Over time, individuals are enveloped in a personalized reality where their own views are constantly reinforced and opposing views are seldom seen. In this environment, users find themselves in a well-furnished echo chamber. This can lead to increased polarization and a diminished capacity for empathetic understanding.

questions.text: What does the underlined phrase "users find themselves in a well-furnished echo chamber" mean?
choices: Users enjoy a comfortable and luxurious online experience.
Users are primarily exposed to information that confirms their existing views.
Users can easily connect with people from different cultural backgrounds.
Users have access to a wide and unfiltered range of information.
Users are actively seeking out debates with those who disagree with them.
questions.explanation: '잘 꾸며진 메아리 방'은 알고리즘에 의해 사용자가 자신의 기존 견해를 확인하고 강화하는 정보에만 주로 노출되어, 다른 관점으로부터 고립되는 상황을 의미합니다.

passages.text: Initially, the small tech startup and the large, established manufacturing firm seemed like unlikely partners. The startup was agile and innovative but lacked capital and market access. The manufacturer was stable and resource-rich but struggled with digital transformation. When they formed a strategic alliance, the results were remarkable. The startup provided cutting-edge software that streamlined the manufacturer's outdated processes, drastically increasing efficiency. In return, the manufacturer provided the funding and global distribution network the startup needed to scale its operations. Neither could have achieved this level of success alone. An industry analyst remarked that their partnership was a perfect example of corporate symbiosis.

questions.text: What does the underlined phrase "a perfect example of corporate symbiosis" mean?
choices: The two companies were engaged in a hostile takeover battle.
The larger company completely absorbed the smaller startup.
The partnership was a temporary arrangement with few long-term benefits.
The companies had a mutually beneficial relationship essential for their success.
One company benefited greatly at the expense of the other.
questions.explanation: '기업 공생'은 생물학의 공생 개념을 비유한 것으로, 서로 다른 두 회사가 각자의 강점을 제공하여 혼자서는 이룰 수 없는 성공을 거두는 상호 이익 관계를 의미합니다.

passages.text: Mark woke up in a terrible mood after a poor night's sleep. He spilled coffee on his shirt, missed his bus, and was late for an important meeting. By the time he got to his desk, he was irritable and pessimistic. His colleague, Sarah, tried to cheer him up with a joke, and his boss praised his latest report, but nothing seemed to help. The initial negativity of his morning had set a tone for the entire day, and he found it incredibly difficult to shift his mindset. Every small annoyance felt like a major catastrophe. It was as if his morning's bad mood had a powerful inertia. He felt stuck in a cycle of frustration, unable to break free.

questions.text: What does the underlined phrase "his morning's bad mood had a powerful inertia" imply?
choices: His bad mood was caused by a physical illness.
He was intentionally trying to remain in a bad mood.
His negative emotional state was resistant to change.
His mood was easily influenced by the people around him.
He quickly forgot the reasons he was upset in the morning.
questions.explanation: '강력한 관성'은 물리학의 관성 개념을 비유한 것입니다. 한번 형성된 나쁜 기분이 외부의 긍정적 자극에도 불구하고 그 상태를 계속 유지하려는 경향, 즉 변화에 저항하는 힘이 강했음을 의미합니다.

passages.text: In 49 B.C., Julius Caesar stood at the bank of the Rubicon, a small river separating his province from the Italian mainland. Roman law forbade any general from crossing it with a standing army. To do so was to declare civil war against the Roman Senate. For a moment, he hesitated, weighing the immense risks against the potential glory. His rivals in Rome had left him no other choice, cornering him politically. After a period of deep contemplation, he gave the order to his legions to advance, famously declaring, "Alea iacta est" (The die is cast). This single act irrevocably set him on a path to either absolute power or a traitor’s death. It was a moment of no return, a gamble for the very soul of the Republic.

questions.text: What does the underlined phrase “Alea iacta est” imply?
choices: He was asking for a sign from the gods before proceeding.
He believed the outcome of the war would be decided by chance.
He had made an irreversible decision and was committed to his course of action.
He was announcing a new set of rules for the game of war.
He was expressing regret over a choice that was forced upon him.
questions.explanation: "주사위는 던져졌다"는 일단 시작하면 되돌릴 수 없는 중대한 결정을 내렸으며, 그 결과에 모든 것을 걸고 자신의 행동 방침에 전념하겠다는 강력한 의지를 나타냅니다.

passages.text: In our hyper-connected world, personal data has become a valuable commodity. Corporations and governments alike collect vast amounts of information about our daily habits, from online purchases to social media interactions. While this data is often used for targeted advertising or urban planning, the potential for misuse is significant. Many citizens feel a growing unease, a sense that their private lives are no longer their own. They install privacy blockers and use encrypted messaging apps, but the feeling of being watched persists. A friend recently remarked, after seeing an ad for a product he had only spoken about, "It's getting harder to believe that Big Brother isn't watching you."

questions.text: What is the implication of the underlined phrase “Big Brother isn't watching you”?
choices: A family member is offering protection and guidance.
One's actions are being constantly monitored by an unseen, powerful entity.
The government is providing enhanced security for its citizens.
It is a sarcastic reference to a popular reality TV show.
People are becoming overly paranoid without any real cause.
questions.explanation: '빅 브라더'는 조지 오웰의 소설 '1984'에서 유래한 상징으로, 보이지 않는 강력한 존재(정부나 거대 기업 등)에 의해 개인의 모든 행동이 끊임없이 감시당하고 있다는 불안감을 의미합니다.

passages.text: Dr. Aris Thorne had spent a decade developing a new theory of cellular regeneration that challenged decades of established doctrine. When he presented his findings at a major conference, the reaction was not excitement, but hostility. The older, tenured professors in the audience dismissed his data, clinging to the familiar paradigms they had built their careers on. They saw his work not as a breakthrough, but as an attack. Frustrated, Dr. Thorne confided in a colleague, "Explaining this to them is like trying to show the sun to the prisoners in Plato's Cave." He knew his path to acceptance would be long and arduous.

questions.text: What does Dr. Thorne mean by the underlined phrase “show the sun to the prisoners in Plato's Cave”?
choices: He is trying to reveal a profound truth to people who are comfortable in their ignorance.
He believes his audience is being held captive against their will.
He is attempting to simplify a complex idea using a famous philosophical allegory.
He feels his research is as brilliant and vital as the sun itself.
He is accusing his colleagues of deliberately hiding the truth from the public.
questions.explanation: 플라톤의 '동굴의 비유'에서 죄수들은 그림자만을 진실로 믿습니다. 이 비유는 기존의 익숙한 무지에 안주하는 사람들에게 새로운 진실을 보여주고 설득하는 것이 얼마나 어려운지를 나타냅니다.

passages.text: After moving to Hollywood with dreams of becoming a screenwriter, Mark found himself working two low-wage jobs just to afford his tiny, windowless apartment. His days were a blur of serving coffee and parking cars, leaving him no time or energy to write. His laptop, filled with half-finished scripts, gathered dust in a corner. One evening, while looking at a pile of unpaid bills on his kitchen table, he let out a bitter laugh. A friend from his hometown called and asked how he was doing. Mark looked around his cramped room and replied with a heavy dose of sarcasm, "Oh, you know. Just living the American Dream."

questions.text: What does Mark actually mean when he says he is “living the American Dream”?
choices: He is genuinely happy and satisfied with his new life in Hollywood.
He is being ironic, meaning his reality is the complete opposite of success and prosperity.
He remains optimistic that he will soon achieve his goals.
He is criticizing the film industry for being too competitive.
He has found a different, more modest version of happiness.
questions.explanation: "아메리칸 드림을 살고 있다"는 말은 반어법입니다. 자신의 비참하고 꿈과 거리가 먼 현실이, 성공과 번영이라는 이상적인 '아메리칸 드림'과 정반대임을 비꼬아 표현하는 것입니다.

passages.text: A recent advertisement for a high-end electric vehicle rarely mentioned its battery range or horsepower. Instead, it featured stunning visuals of a confident, stylish individual driving along a scenic coastal highway, attending exclusive art gallery openings, and working in a sleek, minimalist office. The voiceover spoke of "sculpting your journey" and "defining tomorrow." An advertising analyst commented on the campaign's strategy, noting that the company understood its target audience perfectly. "They are not just moving metal," she explained. "They are selling an aspirational lifestyle." The goal is to make consumers feel that by owning the car, they are buying into a more sophisticated and successful version of themselves.

questions.text: What does the analyst mean by “selling an aspirational lifestyle”?
choices: The company is marketing the car by associating it with a desirable, high-status way of life.
The car is sold with a complete package including a house and a job.
The advertisement is primarily aimed at existing successful people.
The company's main business is lifestyle coaching, not car manufacturing.
The car's features are designed to inspire the driver to be more ambitious.
questions.explanation: '열망의 대상이 되는 라이프스타일을 판다'는 것은 제품의 기능이 아니라, 그 제품을 소유함으로써 얻을 수 있다고 여겨지는 바람직하고 높은 지위의 생활 방식과 이미지를 마케팅하는 것을 의미합니다.

passages.text: The small tech startup, Innovatech, was struggling to compete with the industry giant, OmniCorp. So, when OmniCorp’s star vice president of strategy, David Chen, unexpectedly applied for a position at Innovatech, the CEO was ecstatic. David brought with him brilliant ideas and an impressive track record. However, the company's co-founder, a cautious and experienced engineer, was wary. She had seen OmniCorp use aggressive tactics to crush smaller rivals before. During a board meeting to approve David's hiring, she voiced her concern, stating, "His resume is impeccable, but we must be certain he is not a Trojan horse sent to disrupt us from within."

questions.text: What does the co-founder mean by calling David Chen “a Trojan horse”?
choices: He is a valuable gift that will ensure the company's success.
He is a deceptive person intended to cause the company's downfall from the inside.
He is an outsider who may not fit into the company's culture.
He is a demanding employee who will be difficult to manage.
He is a temporary hire who will likely return to the rival company.
questions.explanation: '트로이 목마'는 겉으로는 이로운 선물처럼 보이지만, 실제로는 내부에서부터 조직을 파괴하기 위해 위장하여 침투한 위험한 존재를 의미하는 고전적인 비유입니다.

passages.text: The quarterly earnings report was grim. The company had missed its targets by a wide margin, and the board demanded immediate cost-cutting measures. As the head of the department, Sarah was given the unenviable task of informing her team. She gathered everyone in the conference room, her expression somber. "Team," she began, her voice carefully neutral, "the company is undergoing a significant strategic realignment to ensure long-term viability. As part of this process, we will be saying goodbye to some of our colleagues so they can explore new opportunities." The air in the room grew heavy as the true meaning of her carefully chosen words sank in.

questions.text: What is the actual meaning of the underlined phrase?
choices: Several employees have voluntarily resigned to take better jobs.
The company is actively helping employees find new careers elsewhere.
A number of employees are being laid off from their jobs.
The team is being reorganized, with some members moving to different departments.
The company is closing temporarily and will rehire the staff later.
questions.explanation: "새로운 기회를 탐색하도록 작별을 고한다"는 것은 해고나 권고사직이라는 부정적인 소식을 부드럽게 표현하려는 전형적인 완곡어법입니다. 실제 의미는 일부 직원들이 해고된다는 것입니다.

passages.text: The new central bank governor was a master of ambiguity. In press conferences, she would deliver long, eloquent statements about monetary policy that, upon closer inspection, committed her to no specific course of action. When asked directly if interest rates would rise, she would reply with phrases like, "We will remain vigilant and data-dependent." Pundits and investors were left frustrated, endlessly debating her true intentions. One seasoned financial journalist wrote in his column, "Trying to predict her next move is like trying to interpret the Mona Lisa's smile." This uncertainty, he argued, was creating volatility in the markets.

questions.text: What does the journalist mean by the phrase “interpret the Mona Lisa's smile”?
choices: To analyze something that is famously enigmatic and open to multiple interpretations.
To appreciate a classic work of art that is universally admired.
To deal with a person who is being deliberately deceptive and untruthful.
To find hidden beauty in a seemingly plain statement.
To uncover a simple truth that is hidden in plain sight.
questions.explanation: '모나리자의 미소를 해석하려는 것'은 그 의미가 무엇인지 명확히 알 수 없어 수수께끼 같고, 여러 가지로 해석될 여지가 있는 대상을 분석하려는 어려운 시도를 비유합니다.

passages.text: In the heart of the Amazon, a team of biologists noticed a disturbing trend. The population of the golden poison frog, a creature known for its sensitivity to environmental changes, was declining at an alarming rate. While other species in the area seemed unaffected for now, the lead scientist, Dr. Elena Ramirez, was deeply concerned. During a video conference with her funding agency, she stressed the urgency of the situation. "This isn't just about one species of frog," she insisted. "This frog is the canary in the coal mine. Its disappearance signals a much larger, systemic threat to the entire rainforest ecosystem."

questions.text: What does Dr. Ramirez mean by calling the frog “the canary in the coal mine”?
choices: It is a rare and valuable species that must be protected for its beauty.
It is an early indicator of a greater, impending danger.
It is a noisy and colorful creature that is easy to monitor.
It is a resilient species that can adapt to many different threats.
It is being used in a scientific experiment to test for toxins.
questions.explanation: '탄광 속의 카나리아'는 임박한 더 큰 위험을 미리 알려주는 조기 경보 신호를 의미하는 관용구입니다. 개구리의 감소가 전체 생태계의 더 큰 위협을 예고한다는 뜻입니다.

passages.text: The small software startup had poured all its resources into developing a revolutionary new app. Their future now hinged entirely on a single presentation to a major venture capital firm. The day after the pitch, the office was silent, the team anxiously awaiting the investors' decision. The founder, a quantum physics enthusiast, paced back and forth. "Right now," he said to his co-founder, "our company is Schrödinger's cat. We are simultaneously a billion-dollar success and a complete failure." He knew that only the investors' phone call would open the box and collapse the wave function, revealing their fate.

questions.text: What does the founder mean by saying the company is “Schrödinger's cat”?
choices: The company's future is in a state of extreme uncertainty, where two opposite outcomes are equally possible until a decisive moment.
The company is engaged in a highly risky and unpredictable scientific experiment.
The company's survival depends on the well-being of a literal cat.
The founder is confused and does not know the current status of his own company.
The company is deliberately hiding its true potential from the public.
questions.explanation: '슈뢰딩거의 고양이'는 관측되기 전까지는 상반된 두 가지 상태가 동시에 존재하는 양자역학의 역설을 비유합니다. 이는 결정적인 순간(투자자의 전화) 전까지 회사의 미래가 극도의 불확실성 속에 놓여 있음을 의미합니다.

passages.text: In the age of social media, many individuals meticulously craft their online personas. They post images of exotic vacations, celebratory dinners, and professional achievements, creating what appears to be a continuous stream of success and happiness. However, this digital facade often conceals the mundane, the difficult, and the disappointing aspects of real life. Friends and followers who consume this content may feel a sense of inadequacy or envy, comparing their own unedited lives to their peers' polished online narratives. This constant comparison can be a significant source of anxiety and dissatisfaction. The platform, designed for connection, ironically becomes a stage for silent competition, where everyone is watching everyone else’s “perfectly curated highlight reel.”

questions.text: What does the underlined phrase “perfectly curated highlight reel” imply?
choices: A documentary that accurately portrays a person's life
A collection of memories that brings genuine joy to others
An intentionally selective and idealized presentation of one's life
A professional portfolio used for job applications
A collaborative project created by a group of friends
questions.explanation: '완벽하게 편집된 하이라이트 영상'은 소셜 미디어상의 모습이 실제 삶의 모든 면이 아닌, 의도적으로 좋은 모습만 선택하고 다듬어서 보여주는 이상화된 결과물임을 의미합니다.

passages.text: The modern professional is often praised for their ability to multitask, juggling emails, meetings, and reports simultaneously. In our information-saturated society, we are constantly connected, and the flow of data is relentless. We have access to more information than any generation before us. Yet, this abundance does not automatically translate into wisdom or clarity. Instead, many find themselves overwhelmed, unable to focus deeply on any single task or piece of information. They skim headlines, glance at notifications, and absorb fragmented pieces of content without ever achieving true understanding. They are, in essence, drowning in the data stream, unable to find the shores of knowledge. This paradox defines the modern intellectual challenge: having access to everything but understanding very little.

questions.text: What is the meaning of “drowning in the data stream” in the context of the passage?
choices: Gaining profound knowledge from various online sources
Being overwhelmed by excessive information and failing to process it meaningfully
Enjoying the process of learning through digital technology
Ignoring the importance of information in the modern world
Becoming an expert in managing and filtering digital data
questions.explanation: '데이터의 흐름 속에서 익사하는 것'은 정보의 홍수 속에서 과도한 정보에 압도되어, 그것을 깊이 있고 의미 있게 처리하지 못하는 상태를 비유적으로 표현합니다.

passages.text: In many corporate environments, the narrative of "hustle culture" is celebrated. Employees who work late, answer emails on weekends, and sacrifice personal time for company projects are often lauded as ideal workers. A manager might pat an exhausted employee on the back and say, “We appreciate your dedication. You’re a true company man.” On the surface, this is a compliment, a recognition of loyalty and hard work. However, beneath this praise lies a darker reality. This culture often leads to burnout, mental health issues, and a severe imbalance between work and life. The compliment, in this context, serves to reinforce a system of exploitation where the individual's well-being is secondary to the corporation's profits. The "company man" is not just loyal; he is consumed.

questions.text: What does the phrase “true company man” ironically suggest in this context?
choices: An employee who is a role model for a healthy work-life balance
A worker who is being exploited under the guise of praise for their dedication
A manager who genuinely cares for the well-being of their staff
An individual who has successfully climbed the corporate ladder
A person who is inefficient and not truly dedicated to their work
questions.explanation: 문맥상 '진정한 회사 인간'이라는 칭찬은 개인의 희생을 강요하는 '허슬 문화'를 정당화하는 말입니다. 따라서 헌신에 대한 칭찬의 탈을 쓰고 실제로는 노동자를 착취하고 있음을 아이러니하게 암시합니다.

passages.text: For decades, many educational systems have operated on a model of knowledge transmission. Teachers, as holders of information, were tasked with pouring facts into the minds of students. Success was measured by how well students could recall and recite these facts on standardized tests. This approach, however, often neglects the development of critical thinking, creativity, and problem-solving skills. It treats the mind as a passive vessel rather than an active muscle to be trained. In an era where information is instantly accessible via the internet, simply knowing facts is less valuable than knowing how to use them. Continuing with this outdated method is like merely watering plastic flowers; the appearance of nurturing is there, but no real growth can ever occur.

questions.text: What does the phrase “merely watering plastic flowers” imply about the educational method described?
choices: It is a creative and effective way to help students grow.
It is a necessary foundation before moving on to more advanced skills.
It is a futile effort that mimics nurturing but produces no genuine intellectual development.
It is a temporary solution for a lack of educational resources.
It is a new and innovative approach to modern education.
questions.explanation: '플라스틱 꽃에 물을 주는 것'은 겉보기에는 교육적인 행위처럼 보이지만 실제로는 아무런 성장이나 결실을 낳지 못하는 헛되고 무의미한 노력을 비유합니다.

passages.text: The environmentalist movement often uses the metaphor of "Spaceship Earth" to convey a critical message about our planet. Imagine humanity as the crew of a vast spaceship, voyaging through the cosmos. This ship has a finite supply of air, water, and food. There are no external service stations or rescue missions. The life support systems are the planet's own ecosystems, which are delicate and interconnected. If the crew pollutes the air, contaminates the water, or exhausts the resources, the ship itself becomes uninhabitable. This perspective forces us to recognize our shared destiny and the urgent need for global cooperation. The actions of one nation can impact the entire crew. In this vast, empty universe, there is no Planet B we can escape to.

questions.text: What is the main point of the underlined statement “there is no Planet B”?
choices: Humanity should focus on finding another habitable planet.
The Earth's resources are infinite and can support us forever.
Technological advancements will eventually solve all environmental problems.
Protecting our current planet is imperative because we have no alternative.
The concept of "Spaceship Earth" is an outdated and pessimistic view.
questions.explanation: "B 행성은 없다"는 말은 우리가 현재 살고 있는 지구 외에는 다른 대안이 없으므로, 현재의 행성을 보호하는 것이 절대적으로 중요하다는 긴급한 메시지를 전달합니다.

passages.text: For centuries, landscape painting was governed by strict rules of realism and perspective. Artists were expected to replicate nature as faithfully as possible. Then came a new generation of painters in the late 19th century who saw the world differently. They were not interested in creating a photographic copy of a scene. Instead, they wanted to capture the feeling, the light, and the momentary impression of a place. One such artist, in his radical departure from tradition, used bold, unnatural colors and distorted forms to express his inner emotional landscape. Critics of his time were appalled, claiming he had no respect for the craft. But in reality, with his revolutionary work, he shattered the canvas of convention.

questions.text: What does it mean that the artist “shattered the canvas” of convention?
choices: He physically destroyed his paintings in a fit of anger.
He completely broke away from the established artistic rules and traditions.
He invented a new type of canvas material for his paintings.
He accidentally ruined his artwork, leading to a new style.
He criticized other artists for being too traditional.
questions.explanation: '관습의 캔버스를 산산조각 냈다'는 것은 예술가가 기존의 예술적 규칙과 전통의 틀을 완전히 깨고 혁신적인 시도를 했음을 비유적으로 표현합니다.

passages.text: Living in a megacity offers unparalleled access to opportunities, culture, and diversity. Millions of people are packed into a dense urban landscape, their lives intersecting in subways, parks, and office buildings. Yet, for many, this physical proximity does not translate into meaningful connection. An individual can live in an apartment building with hundreds of others and not know a single neighbor's name. The sheer scale of the city can create a profound sense of anonymity and isolation. The constant noise and activity become a kind of white noise that masks individual existence. In this environment, one can be surrounded by a crowd yet feel entirely alone, effectively living in a desert of a million people.

questions.text: What does the paradoxical phrase “living in a desert of a million people” highlight?
choices: The lack of water and green spaces in large modern cities
The feeling of extreme loneliness and isolation despite being surrounded by many people
The opportunities for finding solitude and peace within a bustling city
The competitive and hostile environment of urban life
The physical danger of living in a densely populated area
questions.explanation: '백만 명의 사막'이라는 역설적인 표현은 수많은 사람들에게 둘러싸여 있음에도 불구하고, 진정한 연결이 없어 느끼는 극심한 외로움과 고립감을 강조합니다.

passages.text: During the election campaign, the candidate delivered speeches filled with grand visions and sweeping promises. He spoke of a future with no poverty, perfect social harmony, and unprecedented prosperity for all. His rhetoric was powerful, painting vivid pictures of a utopian society that resonated deeply with a hopeful electorate. He assured voters that with his leadership, these ambitious goals were not just possible but inevitable. However, seasoned political analysts were skeptical. They noted the complete lack of concrete policies, feasible timelines, or budget plans to support his claims. To them, the candidate was not presenting a practical roadmap for the future; he was merely building castles in the air.

questions.text: What does the idiom “building castles in the air” suggest about the candidate's promises?
choices: They are based on a solid and well-researched foundation.
They are innovative ideas that will surely be realized in the future.
They are modest goals that are easily achievable.
They are impressive but unrealistic plans with no practical basis.
They are secret plans that will be revealed after the election.
questions.explanation: '공중에 성을 짓는 것'은 실현 불가능한 헛된 계획이나 공상을 의미하는 관용구입니다. 후보자의 약속이 인상적이지만 현실적인 기반이 없음을 시사합니다.

passages.text: Official histories of empires and nations often read like a grand narrative of progress and triumph. They celebrate victorious generals, wise rulers, and glorious battles, constructing a story that legitimizes the present power structure. However, this mainstream account is rarely the whole story. For every celebrated victory, there are untold stories of the vanquished. For every monument built, there are the forgotten laborers who toiled in obscurity. These alternative histories—the experiences of the colonized, the perspectives of the defeated, and the voices of the marginalized—are often ignored, suppressed, or dismissed as unimportant. They exist only as the silent footnotes of history, rarely making it into the main text.

questions.text: What does the phrase “the silent footnotes of history” refer to?
choices: Minor details that are correctly documented but not very interesting
The stories and perspectives of marginalized groups that are largely ignored in official records
Fictional accounts that are added to historical texts to make them more engaging
The primary sources and evidence that historians use for their research
The academic debates and interpretations among historians
questions.explanation: '역사의 조용한 각주'는 공식적인 역사 기록의 주된 서사에서 소외되고 무시되는 소수자나 패배한 자들의 이야기와 관점을 비유적으로 나타냅니다.

passages.text: Many people strive to create a life devoid of risk and uncertainty. They choose stable careers, follow predictable routines, and avoid any situation that might lead to failure or discomfort. From the outside, their life seems secure and well-managed. They have successfully constructed what they believe to be a fortress of comfort. However, this relentless pursuit of safety comes at a cost. By shielding themselves from all potential harm, they also close themselves off from opportunities for growth, adventure, and profound experience. The walls they build to keep danger out also become a prison, preventing them from truly living. The fortress, meant to protect, ultimately confines.

questions.text: What is the ironic implication of the phrase “a fortress of comfort”?
choices: A place that provides both genuine security and personal freedom
A lifestyle that is exciting and full of new challenges
A safe and stable environment that is actually a self-imposed prison limiting personal growth
A temporary shelter from which one can plan future adventures
A financial safety net that allows for taking greater risks
questions.explanation: '편안함의 요새'는 아이러니하게도, 안전을 추구하려 만든 안정적인 환경이 오히려 성장의 기회를 차단하는 스스로 만든 감옥이 되었음을 암시합니다.

passages.text: In the early 20th century, the artist Wassily Kandinsky pioneered a radical shift toward abstraction. He believed that art could be divorced from the depiction of the physical world. For Kandinsky, colors and forms, much like musical notes, possessed an intrinsic spiritual and emotional power. He argued that a specific color could evoke a particular feeling, and a certain shape could resonate with the human soul, all without representing a recognizable object. His theoretical writings, such as "Concerning the Spiritual in Art," laid out his vision for an art that could communicate directly with the viewer's inner world. He sought to develop what could be called a grammar of color, where art could convey profound truths through a purely visual language. This approach fundamentally challenged the traditional foundations of Western art.

questions.text: What is the most likely meaning of the underlined phrase “a grammar of color”?
choices: a rigid and restrictive set of rules for applying paint
a system in which colors and forms function like a language to convey meaning
a scientific analysis of the chemical properties of different pigments
a historical catalog detailing the use of colors in famous paintings
a pedagogical method for teaching children how to mix primary colors
questions.explanation: '색의 문법'은 색과 형태가 단어나 문장처럼 체계적으로 작동하여, 재현 없이도 직접적으로 의미와 감정을 전달하는 시각적 언어 시스템을 의미합니다.

passages.text: Ernest Hemingway's prose is famous for its stark minimalism and deliberate omissions. He developed a literary principle he called the "Iceberg Theory." According to this theory, the factual, explicit details of a story are merely the tip of the iceberg. The true substance, the deeper themes and emotions, lie beneath the surface, unstated but strongly felt. Hemingway believed that by omitting crucial parts of a story, he could make it more powerful. The reader is forced to sense the underlying currents of the narrative, filling in the gaps with their own intuition. This technique gives his work its characteristic weight and resonance, proving that what is left unsaid can be more important than what is written. For him, the strength of a story was in the dignity of movement of an iceberg.

questions.text: What does the underlined phrase “the dignity of movement of an iceberg” imply?
choices: a narrative that progresses at an exceptionally slow and majestic pace
a writing style that is emotionally cold and detached from its characters
the profound impact created by the unstated, implicit elements of the story
a plot that is entirely visible and easily understood by any reader
a literary technique that is inflexible and difficult to adapt
questions.explanation: '빙산의 움직임의 위엄'은 빙산의 대부분이 수면 아래에 있듯이, 이야기의 진정한 힘과 의미는 명시적으로 드러나지 않은 암묵적인 요소에서 나온다는 것을 의미합니다.

passages.text: The Socratic maxim "Know thyself" is often misinterpreted as a simple call for introspection or self-analysis. However, in the context of Socratic philosophy, its meaning is more profound and humbling. For Socrates, the beginning of wisdom was the recognition of one's own ignorance. When the Oracle at Delphi declared him the wisest man, Socrates concluded it was because he alone was aware that he knew nothing. Therefore, the journey of knowing oneself is not about discovering hidden talents or desires, but about understanding the precise limits of one's own knowledge. It is an intellectual pursuit grounded in humility. To truly embark on this path is to accept the Delphic command to 'know thyself' in its most challenging form.

questions.text: What is the most challenging form of “the Delphic command to 'know thyself'” as implied by the passage?
choices: to fully comprehend one's own personality, strengths, and weaknesses
to achieve the highest possible level of academic and factual knowledge
to recognize and accept the boundaries and limitations of one's own wisdom
to blindly follow the prophecies and guidance of a divine authority
to travel the world in order to gain a wide range of experiences
questions.explanation: 소크라테스적 맥락에서 '너 자신을 알라'는 것은 자신의 무지를 깨닫는 것을 의미합니다. 따라서 가장 어려운 형태는 자신의 지혜와 이해의 한계를 인식하고 받아들이는 것입니다.

passages.text: Marshall McLuhan, a Canadian philosopher of communication theory, coined one of the most provocative phrases of the 20th century: "The medium is the message." This aphorism challenges the conventional wisdom that the content of communication is what matters most. McLuhan argued that the characteristics of the medium itself—be it print, radio, television, or the internet—have a more fundamental and pervasive impact on society and human consciousness than the specific information it conveys. For example, the invention of the printing press fostered individualism and linear thinking, while television culture encourages a more simultaneous and collective experience. The technology that delivers information shapes our social organization, our sensory perceptions, and our ways of thinking. In essence, the medium is the message.

questions.text: What is the core meaning of the phrase “the medium is the message”?
choices: The information conveyed by media is far more influential than the technology used.
Media corporations often embed their own biased messages in their content.
The communication channel itself, not its content, is the primary driver of social and psychological change.
To be effective, a message must be carefully tailored to the specific medium being used.
All forms of media, despite their differences, ultimately deliver the same fundamental messages.
questions.explanation: "미디어는 메시지다"는 메시지의 내용보다 그것을 전달하는 매체(미디어 기술) 자체가 사회와 인간의 의식에 더 근본적인 변화를 일으킨다는 핵심 의미를 담고 있습니다.

passages.text: Historian of science Thomas Kuhn fundamentally altered our understanding of scientific progress. In his influential book, "The Structure of Scientific Revolutions," he argued against the traditional view of science as a steady, cumulative acquisition of knowledge. Instead, Kuhn proposed that science undergoes periodic revolutions he called "paradigm shifts." A paradigm is the entire constellation of beliefs, values, and techniques shared by the members of a given scientific community. For long periods, scientists work within an established paradigm, a process Kuhn called "normal science." However, when anomalies accumulate that the current paradigm cannot explain, a crisis ensues, eventually leading to a true paradigm shift—a complete overthrow of the existing framework in favor of a new one, like the shift from Newtonian physics to Einstein's theory of relativity.

questions.text: What does the underlined phrase “a true paradigm shift” refer to?
choices: a slow and steady accumulation of new scientific information
a minor adjustment or correction to an established scientific model
a fundamental and revolutionary overthrow of a discipline's core concepts and worldview
the process of making a complex scientific theory accessible to the general public
a prolonged debate between scientists who support two competing theories
questions.explanation: '진정한 패러다임 전환'은 기존의 과학적 틀이 설명할 수 없는 문제들이 축적될 때, 그 분야의 핵심 개념과 세계관이 근본적이고 혁명적으로 뒤바뀌는 것을 의미합니다.

passages.text: During my travels in rural Japan, I had a chance encounter with an elderly potter in his small, quiet workshop. He spoke no English, and I spoke very little Japanese. Yet, he invited me in for tea. We sat in silence for a while, simply watching the steam rise from our cups. He then showed me a single, perfectly imperfect ceramic bowl, its glaze containing a universe of subtle cracks and colors. We did not exchange many words, but in that brief moment, a profound sense of connection and understanding passed between us. The meeting was fleeting and could never be replicated. It was then that I truly understood the essence of Ichi-go ichi-e. This memory remains more vivid than any famous landmark I visited.

questions.text: What is the most likely meaning of “the essence of Ichi-go ichi-e” in this context?
choices: the necessity of forming long-lasting and stable relationships
the beauty and significance of a unique, unrepeatable moment in time
the profound sadness that comes with saying a final farewell
the cultural importance of showing hospitality to strangers
the exceptional skill and dedication of a master craftsman
questions.explanation: '이치고 이치에'는 '일생에 단 한 번뿐인 만남'을 의미합니다. 문맥상, 다시는 없을 그 순간의 아름다움과 중요성, 그리고 그 안에서 나눈 깊은 교감을 깨달았다는 뜻입니다.

passages.text: In "The Wealth of Nations," Adam Smith introduced a concept that would become a cornerstone of capitalist theory. He observed that an individual, by pursuing their own economic self-interest, frequently promotes the good of society more effectually than when they really intend to promote it. A butcher or a baker does not provide us with our dinner out of benevolence, but from their regard for their own profit. However, in a competitive market, their ambition to succeed drives them to offer better products at lower prices. Smith argued that by pursuing their own gain, they are led by an invisible hand to promote an end which was no part of their intention—the public good. This mechanism, he believed, harmonized private interest with social welfare.

questions.text: What does the phrase “led by an invisible hand” mean?
choices: business activities are secretly controlled and manipulated by the government
economic outcomes are largely determined by random luck and unforeseen chance
individuals often act charitably without being conscious of their generosity
market forces can channel self-interested behavior into socially desirable outcomes
a divine or spiritual power is secretly guiding the fluctuations of the economy
questions.explanation: '보이지 않는 손에 이끌린다'는 것은 개인의 이기적인 행동이 시장의 경쟁과 가격이라는 보이지 않는 메커니즘을 통해 의도치 않게 사회적으로 바람직한 결과를 낳는 것을 의미합니다.

passages.text: The psychologist Carl Jung proposed that the human psyche is composed of both conscious and unconscious elements. One of the most critical components of the unconscious is what he termed "the shadow." The shadow represents the parts of our personality that we choose to repress and hide, aspects that we find shameful, inferior, or unacceptable. It contains our repressed instincts, desires, and weaknesses. Jung argued that ignoring the shadow is dangerous, as it often leads to us projecting these negative traits onto others, blaming them for our own unacknowledged faults. For genuine psychological growth and wholeness, one must turn inward and face these disowned parts. The difficult but necessary task is to integrate one's shadow.

questions.text: What does the underlined phrase “to integrate one's shadow” mean?
choices: to completely eliminate all negative thoughts and behaviors from one's life
to successfully conceal one's personal flaws from others
to project one's own faults onto other people in order to feel superior
to cultivate a mysterious personality that is difficult for others to understand
to consciously acknowledge and accept the repressed and disowned aspects of one's personality
questions.explanation: '그림자를 통합한다'는 것은 자신의 성격 중 억압하고 부인해왔던 어두운 측면들을 외면하거나 타인에게 투사하지 않고, 의식적으로 인정하고 받아들이는 것을 의미합니다.

passages.text: In many Western cultures, the nuclear family is seen as the primary unit responsible for raising children. However, this is not a universal perspective. During her fieldwork in a West African community, an anthropologist observed a profoundly different approach. Here, child-rearing was a collective endeavor. Aunts, uncles, cousins, and even unrelated neighbors played a direct and active role in a child's upbringing, offering guidance, discipline, and support. This communal network ensures that children are embedded in a rich web of relationships and learn social responsibility from a young age. When the anthropologist asked a village elder about this system, the elder smiled and explained, "Here, we know that it takes a village to raise a child."

questions.text: What is the meaning of the proverb “it takes a village to raise a child” in this context?
choices: Children are better off being raised in small, rural communities.
A child's happiness depends on having a very large number of siblings.
The entire community shares the responsibility for a child's development and well-being.
The biological parents are not the most significant figures in a child's life.
The task of raising a child is so difficult that it is almost impossible.
questions.explanation: "아이 하나를 키우려면 온 마을이 필요하다"는 속담은 아이의 양육과 발달이 부모만의 책임이 아니라, 친척, 이웃 등 공동체 전체의 공동 책임이라는 의미를 함축합니다.

passages.text: The relationship between language and thought has long been a subject of debate. The Sapir-Whorf hypothesis offers a compelling, though controversial, perspective. It proposes that the structure of a language affects its speakers' worldview or cognition. The strong version of this hypothesis, linguistic determinism, suggests that language dictates thought. A more widely accepted weaker version, linguistic relativity, posits that language merely influences thought and perception. For example, a language that lacks a future tense might foster a different conception of time in its speakers. From this viewpoint, language is not simply a passive vehicle for pre-existing ideas; it is an active force that shapes how we segment and understand the world. In short, language is not a neutral tool for describing reality.

questions.text: What does the underlined phrase “language is not a neutral tool for describing reality” imply?
choices: Some languages are inherently more logical and precise than others.
People who speak multiple languages have a more objective perception of reality.
The specific language one speaks can shape and influence one's perception of the world.
It is impossible to achieve perfect translation of concepts between different languages.
Reality exists objectively and is completely independent of the language used to describe it.
questions.explanation: "언어는 현실을 묘사하는 중립적인 도구가 아니다"는 말은 사피어-워프 가설의 핵심으로, 우리가 사용하는 특정 언어가 세계에 대한 우리의 인식을 형성하고 영향을 줄 수 있음을 의미합니다.

passages.text: A city council is debating a new environmental initiative. The proposal focuses on increasing the number of recycling bins in public parks and launching a small-scale public awareness campaign. However, it fails to address the massive amounts of industrial waste being dumped into the city's river by several large factories. During the debate, a passionate environmental activist and council member, Ms. Anya Sharma, stands up to speak. She acknowledges the good intentions behind the proposal but concludes her speech by stating, "While I appreciate the effort, this proposal is tantamount to putting a band-aid on a bullet wound." She argues for stricter regulations on corporate polluters and significant investment in water treatment facilities instead.

questions.text: What does Ms. Sharma mean when she says they are "putting a band-aid on a bullet wound"?
choices: The proposal is a necessary first aid measure for a minor issue.
The city should prioritize medical emergencies over environmental ones.
The proposed solution is completely inadequate for the true scale of the problem.
The initiative, though small, will effectively stop the core problem over time.
The factories' pollution is a violent act that requires a police response.
questions.explanation: '총상에 반창고를 붙인다'는 것은 매우 심각한 문제에 대해, 그 문제의 규모에 비해 턱없이 부족하고 피상적인 해결책을 제시하는 상황을 비유합니다.

passages.text: In a recent economic analysis report, a leading economist, Dr. Evans, expressed concern about the nation's overall economic health. While major stock market indices remained stable, he pointed to a different, more subtle indicator: the sharp increase in bankruptcies among small, independent bookstores and coffee shops. He wrote, "These small businesses, often operating on thin margins, are highly sensitive to shifts in consumer spending and local economic vitality. Their recent struggles are not isolated incidents; they are the canaries in the coal mine, signaling a potential downturn that could soon affect the broader economy." He urged policymakers to look beyond the headline numbers and address the foundational issues affecting small enterprises.

questions.text: What does Dr. Evans mean by calling the struggling small businesses "the canaries in the coal mine"?
choices: They are a small and insignificant part of the national economy.
They are an early warning sign of a much larger, impending problem.
They are resilient businesses that can survive any economic condition.
They are creating a toxic environment for larger corporations.
They are a unique phenomenon that will not be repeated in other sectors.
questions.explanation: '탄광 속의 카나리아'는 임박한 더 큰 위험을 미리 알려주는 조기 경보 신호를 의미하는 관용구입니다. 소규모 자영업의 위기가 더 큰 경제 침체의 전조라는 뜻입니다.

passages.text: A documentary on marine biology explored the intricate ecosystem of the Pacific kelp forests. The narrator explained that while the forest is home to myriad species, the sea otter plays a uniquely crucial role. By preying on sea urchins, which graze voraciously on kelp, the otters prevent the urchin population from exploding and destroying the kelp beds. The lead scientist interviewed in the film stated, "Many creatures depend on the kelp for food and shelter. The sea otter, by controlling the urchins, ensures the forest's survival. It is not just another resident; it is the keystone in the arch of this marine ecosystem." Without the otters, the entire structure would collapse, leading to a barren underwater landscape.

questions.text: What does the scientist mean by calling the sea otter "the keystone in the arch of this marine ecosystem"?
choices: The sea otter is the most common animal in the kelp forest.
The sea otter's presence is structurally essential for the stability of the entire ecosystem.
The sea otter is physically shaped like an architectural element.
The sea otter is a predator at the very top of the food chain.
The sea otter's role is primarily decorative and symbolic.
questions.explanation: '아치의 쐐기돌'은 전체 구조를 지탱하는 핵심 부분을 비유합니다. 해달의 존재가 생태계 전체의 구조적 안정성을 위해 필수적이라는 의미입니다.

passages.text: In a performance review meeting, Sarah, a manager, was discussing a new project with her subordinate, Tom. Tom had been consistently working overtime for weeks to cover for a colleague who had abruptly quit. Sarah, overlooking his recent efforts, assigned him another urgent, high-stakes task. After laying out the demanding requirements, she asked, "So, are you on board, Tom? We need a real team player for this." Tom paused for a moment, forced a tight smile, and replied in a perfectly even tone, "I'm just glad someone with your clear vision is leading the charge." He then quietly accepted the assignment documents.

questions.text: What does Tom's statement most likely imply?
choices: He genuinely admires his manager's leadership and strategic direction.
He is sarcastically expressing his frustration with his manager's unfair demands.
He is excited about the new project and the opportunity to prove himself.
He is subtly suggesting that he should be the one leading the project.
He is confused about the project's vision and is asking for clarification.
questions.explanation: 톰의 발언은 겉보기와 달리, 자신의 노고를 무시하고 무리한 요구를 하는 관리자의 '명확한 비전'을 비꼬며 좌절감을 풍자적으로 표현하는 것입니다.

passages.text: A cultural critic wrote an article about the evolving identity of a major metropolitan city. For decades, the city was described as a "melting pot," where immigrants were expected to assimilate and adopt a homogenized new culture. However, the critic argues this metaphor is no longer accurate. She observes vibrant neighborhoods where different languages, cuisines, and traditions flourish alongside each other, contributing to the city's overall character without losing their distinctiveness. She concludes, "To truly understand this city, we must see it not as a melting pot that erases differences, but as a magnificent tapestry woven from a thousand different threads." Each thread, she explains, retains its own color and texture while being integral to the beauty of the whole.

questions.text: What does the author mean by describing the city as "a magnificent tapestry woven from a thousand different threads"?
choices: The city is a chaotic and disorganized collection of different groups.
The city's various cultures blend together to form one single, new culture.
The city is beautiful because individual cultures coexist and contribute their unique qualities to the whole.
The city's identity is fragile and can easily be unraveled.
The city's cultural landscape is primarily a visual and artistic experience.
questions.explanation: '웅장한 태피스트리'는 다양한 문화들이 각자의 고유성을 잃지 않으면서 함께 어우러져 도시 전체의 아름답고 조화로운 정체성을 형성한다는 것을 비유합니다.

passages.text: The CEO of a once-promising tech startup, "Innovatech," held a press conference to announce the company's bankruptcy. Just two years prior, Innovatech had received massive funding for its ambitious project: a global network of solar-powered delivery drones. The project was plagued by regulatory hurdles, immense technological challenges, and costs that spiraled far beyond initial projections. Critics had warned that the technology was not yet viable on such a scale. In his somber speech, the CEO reflected on their journey, stating, "We dreamed of changing the world. We pushed the boundaries of what was thought possible. But in our ambition, we flew too close to the sun."

questions.text: What does the CEO mean by saying "we flew too close to the sun"?
choices: They were on the verge of a major scientific breakthrough using solar power.
Their failure was caused by excessive and reckless ambition that ignored clear warnings.
The company's operations were negatively affected by unusually hot weather.
They were betrayed by their investors who were too demanding.
They succeeded in reaching their ultimate goal, but at a great personal cost.
questions.explanation: '태양에 너무 가까이 날았다'는 이카루스 신화에 빗대어, 경고를 무시한 과도하고 무모한 야망이 결국 파멸적인 실패를 초래했음을 의미합니다.

passages.text: In his book on 20th-century diplomacy, a historian analyzes the tense negotiations between two rival nations in 1962. For weeks, diplomats had exchanged proposals, but a significant gap remained. The first nation demanded the complete removal of military bases, while the second offered only a partial reduction. Finally, the lead diplomat for the first nation delivered a final, non-negotiable ultimatum to his counterpart. He stated that if the bases were not fully dismantled within 48 hours, all diplomatic ties would be severed permanently. The historian writes, "This declaration was not another bargaining chip. For the diplomat and his country, this was their crossing of the Rubicon."

questions.text: What does the historian mean by "their crossing of the Rubicon"?
choices: They were making a final, desperate attempt to find a peaceful compromise.
They were retreating from their previous position to avoid conflict.
They had made an irreversible decision from which there was no turning back.
They were violating an established geographical and political boundary.
They were proposing a secret meeting to resolve the issue quietly.
questions.explanation: '루비콘 강을 건너다'는 카이사르의 일화에서 유래한 것으로, 더 이상 되돌릴 수 없는 중대한 결정을 내리고 그 결과에 모든 것을 걸었음을 의미합니다.

passages.text: An art critic is reviewing a new exhibition by a minimalist painter. The gallery walls are mostly bare, featuring only a few canvases. Each canvas contains just one or two simple geometric shapes painted in a single, muted color against a vast white background. Some visitors walk through quickly, confused by the apparent emptiness. However, the critic argues for a deeper appreciation. She writes, "The artist challenges our perception of art. He is not interested in filling the space, but in activating it. The true subject is not the black square, but the sea of white around it. The power of his work lies in the eloquence of the negative space."

questions.text: What does the critic mean by "the eloquence of the negative space"?
choices: The artist's work is powerful because it is silent and communicates nothing.
The empty or unpainted areas of the canvas are crucial for conveying the artwork's meaning.
The paintings evoke negative emotions like sadness and loneliness in the viewer.
The artist made a mistake by leaving too much of the canvas blank.
The true meaning of the art can only be understood by its absence from the gallery.
questions.explanation: '네거티브 스페이스의 웅변'은 그림에서 비어 있는 공간(여백)이 형태가 있는 부분만큼이나 작품의 의미와 효과를 전달하는 데 중요한 역할을 한다는 것을 의미합니다.

passages.text: In a complex white-collar crime trial, the prosecution presented a mountain of circumstantial evidence against the defendant, a chief financial officer accused of embezzling funds. They showed unusual financial transfers, lavish spending habits, and deleted company records. However, the defense team argued that none of these pieces of evidence constituted direct proof of guilt. The turning point came when a forensic analyst recovered a deleted email from the defendant's computer. The email, sent to an offshore bank, contained instructions matching the exact time and amount of a missing corporate fund. In her closing argument, the prosecutor held up a printout of the email and declared, "This email may seem like one small document, but in the context of everything else we have shown you, it is the straw that broke the camel's back."

questions.text: What does the prosecutor mean by "the straw that broke the camel's back"?
choices: This single piece of evidence is too weak to be considered seriously.
The defendant's crime is as burdensome as carrying a large amount of straw.
This final piece of evidence, when added to all the others, makes the defendant's guilt undeniable.
The evidence was discovered by chance, much like finding a piece of straw.
The defense team's arguments were flimsy and weak, like straw.
questions.explanation: '낙타의 등을 부러뜨린 지푸라기'는 여러 정황 증거가 쌓인 상태에서, 사소해 보이는 마지막 증거 하나가 결정타가 되어 피고인의 유죄를 부인할 수 없게 만들었음을 의미합니다.

passages.text: A young entrepreneur, Maria, was devastated after her first business venture failed. She confessed to her mentor, an experienced businessman named David, that she felt like a complete failure and was afraid to try anything new. David listened patiently and then shared stories of his own early struggles, including two bankruptcies. He told her, "Success is not a clean, straight line. It's a messy, winding path. Don't hide from your failure; study it. Understand what went wrong and why. You must learn to see that a master's scars are his medals." He encouraged her to view her recent experience not as a final judgment, but as the most valuable part of her education.

questions.text: What does David mean when he says "a master's scars are his medals"?
choices: True masters never make mistakes or fail.
The physical injuries one sustains are proof of hard work.
